macro_segments.title: Introduction to the Mishna's Two Explanations: Shachiv Mera and Maamad Shloshtan

First Explanation: Shachiv Mera (Deathbed Bequest) and Its Legal Basis
Second Explanation: Maamad Shloshtan (Three Parties Present)
The Mechanism Problem: How Does a Debt Transfer Between Three Parties?

macro_segments.title: Mar Zutra's Position: Three Rabbinic Enactments Without Clear Mechanism

micro_segments.title: Mar Zutra's Teaching: Three Things Made into Halachos Without Reason (Kelachta Belo Taama)
Tosafot's Interpretation: No Clear Mechanism but Beneficial Purpose
Three Rabbinic Enactments: Maamad Shloshtan, Wife as Apotropa, and Chuppah Transaction
Details of the Three Enactments and Their Logical Basis

macro_segments.title: Tosafot's Questions on Maamad Shloshtan: Refusal and Non-Jewish Parties

micro_segments.title: Tosafot's First Question: Does Maamad Shloshtan Work if One Party Refuses?
Tosafot's Second Question: Does Maamad Shloshtan Work with Non-Jewish Parties?

macro_segments.title: Rav's Teaching with Saffron: Maamad Shloshtan in Practice

micro_segments.title: Rav's Statement to Rav Acha Bardla: Transfer of Saffron as Maamad Shloshtan
The Gemara's Question: Why Repeat a Known Principle?
The Gemara's Answer: Small vs. Large Gifts and the Need for All Three Parties

macro_segments.title: The Gardeners' Case: Maamad Shloshtan with Error and Kinyan

micro_segments.title: The Gardeners Settle Their Accounts: Transferring Debt to Landowner
Discovery of Mathematical Error and Request to Rav Nachman
Rav Nachman's Ruling: Binding Nature of Maamad Shloshtan and Kinyan
Rava's Objection: Kinyan Based on Mistaken Assumption

macro_segments.title: Holich (Delivery) vs. Ten (Give): Liability and Retraction Rights

micro_segments.title: The Question: Does Holich (Deliver) Create the Same Rights as Ten (Give)?
Rav's Position: Holich Kizachi - Partial Possession, Debt Not Paid
Shmuel's Objection: No Middle Position - All or Nothing
The Debate Explained: Whether a Middle Position of Possession is Possible
Braita Supporting Rav: Holich and Ten Both Create Non-Retractable Status

macro_segments.title: The Pikadon Case: Can the Creditor Reclaim When Messenger Holds Deposit?

micro_segments.title: Question About Pikadon: Does Creditor's Claim Override the Messenger's Holding?
Rav Zeira's Answer: Exception When the Creditor Previously Denied Ownership

macro_segments.title: Rav Sheshet's Debt Collection: Messenger's Role and Liability Refusal

micro_segments.title: Rav Sheshet's Coats and Debts: Instructing Rav Yosef to Collect
Debtors Demand Kinyan; Messenger Refuses and Avoids Agreement
Rav Sheshet's Praise: Messenger Should Not Assume Debtor's Liability

macro_segments.title: Rav Achai's Goblet Case: The Shaliach's Authority and Practical Pressure

micro_segments.title: Rav Achai Sends Rabbis to Retrieve His Silver Goblet from Nahardea
Holders Demand Kinyan; One Rabbi Refuses, One Acquiesces Under Pressure
Confrontation and Justification: Self-Defense Against Powerful Intimidators
Lesson: Legal Rights Must Be Balanced Against Practical Circumstances

macro_segments.title: Holich Kizachi Debate: Does Recipient Acquire Immediately Upon Command to Deliver?

micro_segments.title: The Case: Sender Commands Delivery to Recipient Who Then Dies
Debate Formulated: Does Holich Kizachi? (Does recipient acquire immediately?)
Abba bar Memel's Resolution: Holich is Not Kizachi; Works Only via Deathbed or Mitzvah Lekayeim
Case Distinction: When Recipient Was Alive at Time of Command (Shichiv Mera)
Rav Pappa's Approach: Holich Works via Mitzvah Lekayeim Divrei Hameis (Fulfilling Deceased's Wishes)
Conclusion: Holich Lav Kizachi in Normal Circumstances; Transfers via Special Halachos

topical_tags.term: Kelachta Belo Taama (Halacha without Clear Mechanism)
Zachin Leadam Shelo Befanav (Acquiring for Another)
Mitzvah Lekayeim Divrei Hameis (Obligation to Fulfill Deceased's Wishes)
Rav Huna in Name of Rav
